evals.prompt: Write a Drupal service class called UserProfileManager in the Drupal\my_module namespace. It needs EntityTypeManagerInterface and AccountProxyInterface as dependencies. Add a method getCurrentUserProfile() that loads the current user's profile. Include the complete PHP file.
evals.expected_output: A service class using constructor injection with typed dependencies, not \Drupal:: static calls anywhere in the class body.
expectations: Uses constructor injection for all dependencies
Generated PHP passes php -l syntax check

evals.prompt: Write the .services.yml file and the corresponding PHP service class for a Drupal service called my_module.report_generator. It depends on entity_type.manager, database (Drupal\Core\Database\Connection), and logger.channel.my_module. Show both files.
evals.expected_output: A .services.yml with proper service definition using '@' prefix for service references, and a PHP class with interface type-hints in the constructor.
expectations: services.yml uses '@entity_type.manager' syntax for service references
PHP class constructor uses EntityTypeManagerInterface (not EntityTypeManager concrete class)
PHP class constructor uses Connection type-hint for database
Generated PHP passes php -l syntax check

evals.prompt: Write a Drupal service class called CacheWarmer in Drupal\my_module namespace with CacheBackendInterface and ConfigFactoryInterface as dependencies. It should have a warmCache(): void method. Include the full PHP file.
evals.expected_output: A final class with constructor property promotion using private readonly for dependencies.
expectations: Uses constructor property promotion (private readonly in constructor parameters)
Does NOT have separate property declarations and manual assignment in constructor body
Generated PHP passes php -l syntax check

evals.prompt: Write a Drupal service class called TokenValidator in Drupal\my_module namespace. It depends on StateInterface and TimeInterface. It should have a method isTokenValid(string $token): bool. Use modern PHP patterns. Include the full PHP file.
evals.expected_output: A class using constructor property promotion with private readonly modifiers, declared as final.
expectations: Uses 'private readonly' in constructor parameter list (property promotion)
Generated PHP passes php -l syntax check
